source_references: S.I. No. 639/2019 (Public Service Pay and Pensions Act 2017 (Payments to Community Pharmacy Contractors) Regulations 2019), Article 2 — definition of "ingredient cost"
Framework Agreement on the Supply and Pricing of Medicines (FASPM) 2026-2029
interpretation_text: The PCRS druglist column "Reimbursement Price" is the ingredient cost as defined in S.I. 639/2019 Article 2: the ex-factory price plus a wholesale mark-up of 8 per cent (12 per cent for fridge items). The ex-factory price itself is defined in FASPM as the price-to-wholesaler exclusive of VAT. The published Reimbursement Price therefore includes the regulated wholesale margin and excludes VAT, pharmacy mark-up, and dispensing/care fees (paid separately under GMS, DPS, LTI and other schemes). It is mapped to comparison_category=pharmacy_purchase_price.
includes_margin: wholesale margin only (8%, 12% for fridge items); excludes pharmacy mark-up and dispensing/care fees
caveats: PCRS publishes ~12.5% of rows with Reimbursement Price=0.0 (May 2026 download); these are typically High Tech medicines (separate scheme), special-claim items, or items priced via reference-pricing arrangements where the published value is held at zero — exclude from headline aggregates.

The Reimbursement Price is the gross figure; HSE retains rebate amounts under FASPM clauses (e.g., 9%/7%/6%/4% over 2026-2029 for new on-patent medicines). Net amounts paid by HSE are not published per item.
Reference-priced items (PCRS column "Reference Priced"=Y) have their reimbursement capped at the reference price; this can decouple the published Reimbursement Price from the actual paid amount.
The PCRS druglist is the non-High-Tech scheme; High Tech medicines are published separately as the "List of Prescribable High Tech Medicines" and are governed by a different scheme.

source_references: S.I. No. 639/2019, Article 2 — definition of ingredient cost
NCPE Guidelines for Inclusion of Drug Costs in Pharmacoeconomic Evaluations v3.4 (April 2026), Section 3
interpretation_text: Derived ex-factory price = PCRS Reimbursement Price ÷ (1 + wholesale markup). High Tech drugs: 8% markup on ALL items (NCPE v3.4 Section 3). Community non-fridge (oral tablets, capsules): 8% (S.I. 639/2019 Art. 2(b)). Community fridge items (injections): 12% (S.I. 639/2019 Art. 2(a)). Maps to comparison_category=manufacturer_price. Directly comparable to PL Cena zbytu netto and CZ cenaPuvodce (all ex-VAT, ex-factory).
adjudication_notes: Fridge classification is best-effort based on dosage form (injection → fridge). Some oral biologics may be misclassified.
caveats: Fridge/non-fridge classification is heuristic: injection/infusion forms assumed fridge (12%), all others 8%. Some edge cases may be misclassified.
High Tech drugs are uniformly 8% regardless of storage requirements (confirmed NCPE v3.4).
Net prices paid by HSE are lower due to FASPM rebates (9%/7%/6%/5% over 2026-2029 for patented exclusives), but these are confidential and not reflected here.

notes: Fridge/non-fridge classification is heuristic: injection/infusion forms assumed fridge (12%), all others 8%. Some edge cases may be misclassified.
High Tech drugs are uniformly 8% regardless of storage requirements (confirmed NCPE v3.4).
Net prices paid by HSE are lower due to FASPM rebates (9%/7%/6%/5% over 2026-2029 for patented exclusives), but these are confidential and not reflected here.
injectable, infusible, or parenteral rows use the statutory 12% wholesale markup reversal

legal_basis: NCPE Guidelines for Inclusion of Drug Costs in Pharmacoeconomic Evaluations v3.4 Section 3
caveats: Fridge/non-fridge classification is approximated from normalised dosage form and route when explicit storage flags are unavailable.
